summary: Iter6 — Tested new 'employee' role + ExchangeRate.real_rate + GET /api/admin/revenue. Added /app/backend/tests/test_employee_and_revenue.py (26 tests, all passing). Full backend regression: 106 passed / 1 skipped (the pre-existing iter1 VIP withdraw-refund skip). Frontend Playwright verified: admin sees 'Ingresos' link & AdminRevenue page (real profit calculation 8.1 USDT over 20 USD→CUP orders, real_rate=410, margin=3.65%), period filter works, employee does NOT see Ingresos link nor render the page, normal users are redirected to /dashboard, AdminRates dialog has rate-real input.

minor.issue: Only POST is implemented at /api/admin/products (the public /api/products handles GET). The review request listed GET /api/admin/products as employee-accessible, but the endpoint returns 405. Listing is via /api/products (public). Not a regression — works as in iter5. Adjusted test accordingly.

Persists as null OR missing the field on returned doc depending on Pydantic model_dump exclude. Verified value None is accepted and lookup later returns None — passes test. No action needed but worth confirming GET shape consistency.

by_role bucket counts volume even when real_rate is missing (volume always counted), but profit_usdt only when rate exists. Documented behavior; consider also exposing 'profit-eligible orders' count per role for accuracy.

ui_bugs.issue: Iter6 request asks for a 'Real' column in the rates table next to Normal/VIP. The dialog has the rate-real field (data-testid='rate-real') and persists correctly, but the table headers are still only Par/Normal/VIP/Actualizada — no Real column. Users can't see at a glance which pairs have real_rate configured.

action_items: (MEDIUM) Add a 'Real' column to the AdminRates table (after VIP) so the configured real_rate is visible without opening the edit dialog — currently only visible in the dialog and in /admin/revenue. The data is already returned by GET /api/rates.
(LOW) Consider 405 handling for /api/admin/products GET — either implement it for symmetry with other resources, or document that listing is via the public /api/products endpoint.
(LOW, carry-over from iter5) Field(ge=0) on AdminSettings.vip_threshold_usdt.
(LOW, carry-over) Align redemption-reject and withdrawal-reject refunds with source field.
critical_code_review_comments: /app/backend/server.py is now 1174 lines — still well past the 700-line guideline. Strongly recommend splitting routers (auth, orders, withdrawals, redemptions, admin, push, revenue).
require_staff() correctly enforces role∈{admin,employee}; update_user() correctly blocks employee→admin/employee promotion (server.py L1111).
_compute_order_profit() correctly returns None when real_rate is missing — and admin_revenue() then skips by_pair contribution but still counts volume. Sound design.
by_role bucket uses 'vip' when user_role∈{vip,admin}; otherwise 'normal'. Employees creating orders fall into 'normal' bucket — matches iter6 spec (employees are not VIP).
AdminRates table missing 'Real' column despite the iter6 spec mandating it. Field exists in dialog only (data-testid='rate-real').
updated_files: /app/backend/tests/test_employee_and_revenue.py (NEW — 26 tests covering: employee /auth/me, /api/admin/revenue access matrix (admin 200, employee/vip/normal 403, no-auth 401), days filter, response shape, real_rate CRUD via employee, employee access to staff endpoints (orders/withdrawals/redemptions/stats/users/seed/push), employee restrictions (cannot update /admin/settings, cannot promote to admin/employee, can set normal/vip), admin can promote to employee, end-to-end profit computation USD→CUP rate_vip=395 real_rate=410 amount=100 → profit_to=1500 CUP appears in by_pair, missing real_rate pair appears in missing_real_rate_pairs).

success_rate.backend: 100% (26/26 new + 80/80 carried over; 1 unchanged pre-existing skip)
success_rate.frontend: 7/8 verified (the 1 ambiguous test was a selector false-positive — restriction logic in AdminUsers is correct via static review)

test_credentials: Admin: test_session_admin_X (user_test_admin01). VIP: test_session_vip_X. Normal: test_session_normal_X. Employee (NEW): test_session_employee_X (user_test_employee01, email employee.test@resilience.com).
seed_data_creation: Inserted db.users{user_id:'user_test_employee01', role:'employee'} and db.user_sessions{session_token:'test_session_employee_X'} via mongosh — required for testing the new role. Documented above. Admin tests left USD→CUP rate with rate_normal=380/rate_vip=395/real_rate=410.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iter6 added: employee role + real_rate column on ExchangeRate + /api/admin/revenue endpoint (admin-only). Full backend regression 106/107 (1 unchanged skip). Frontend AdminRevenue page works end-to-end (verified visually: 8.1 USDT profit over 20 orders, USD→CUP). One minor frontend gap: AdminRates table does not show a 'Real' column even though the dialog has the rate-real field — main agent should add the column. Auth: 4 tokens available — admin, vip, normal, employee. Re-run: `pytest /app/backend/tests/test_employee_and_revenue.py -v` (~8s).
rca of the issue: No critical bugs found. The only frontend gap is the missing 'Real' column in AdminRates table — purely additive UI work (data already comes from GET /api/rates as r.real_rate). Recommended fix: add `Real` between VIP and Actualizada in /app/frontend/src/pages/admin/AdminRates.jsx and a corresponding `{r.real_rate ?? '—'}` in the row map. Backend behavior is fully spec-compliant; the e2e profit computation test created a VIP order USD→CUP amount=100 → amount_to=39500 (rate_vip=395, no commission), approved it, and verified USD→CUP appears in by_pair with positive profit_to (≥1500), VIP role bucket orders incremented, and the pair is NOT in missing_real_rate_pairs.
